changes_made.description: Replaced verbose 573-line original skill with concise optimized version. Removed irrelevant SQL/visualization content, scripts/, and sample CSV. Added focused CSV processing, aggregation, cleaning, and multi-dataset patterns with Python code templates.

Added CRITICAL RULES section emphasizing: always write Python scripts (never manual computation), use exact field names, only dedup when prompted. Fixed multi-dataset-report revenue error (model had manually written 29750 instead of 28750).

Restructured data cleaning section with complete copy-paste script template showing dedup-first then count-on-unique workflow. Added explicit instruction that counts must be on unique rows, not all rows.
Added stronger WARNING about not iterating over `rows` for counts. Explained that task prompt's 'before dedup' means counting distinct records, not all rows including duplicates.
Added explicit DO NOT instruction in WARNING block. Model continues to follow task prompt's 'before dedup' literally over skill instruction.

notes: data-clean persistently scores 85/100 because qwen3.5-397b follows the task prompt's explicit 'counted before dedup' instruction over the skill's contrary dedup-first counting methodology. The model counts dates_standardized=7 (on all 15 rows) instead of 5 (on 12 unique rows). This appears to be a fundamental limitation where the model prioritizes the direct task instruction over skill guidance.
